steps.title: Combine fractions on the left side with common denominator
steps.content: The LCD of 2 and 6 is 6. Rewrite each term: $$\frac{3}{6}x - \frac{1}{6}x = 1$$ Combine the fractions: $$\frac{2}{6}x = 1$$ Simplify: $$\frac{1}{3}x = 1$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content: Multiply both sides by 3 to isolate $x$: $$x = 3$$ That confirms C is correct.
